workItem.title: Aggregate high-frequency Codex progress telemetry

## Context

A recovered bos-front AgentRun produced hundreds of item/agentMessage/delta AuditEvents in seconds. Persisting every text delta as a permanent event inflates the audit branch, exhausts API budget and contradicts the retention policy that progress telemetry is aggregated.

## Expected outcome

Symphony preserves durable milestones, commands, tools, errors and final agent messages while aggregating or sampling high-frequency progress deltas into bounded summaries with explicit counts and time windows.

## Scope

- Classify App Server notifications by evidentiary value
- Aggregate agent message deltas and token/progress updates in bounded windows
- Keep errors, command/tool boundaries, reviews and final messages lossless
- Record aggregate counts and dropped/sampled event classes
- Add volume and recovery regression tests
- Document retention behavior

## Out of scope

- Discard command results or errors
- Store private model reasoning
- Change permanent evidence contracts

## Acceptance criteria

- A streamed agent message cannot create one permanent AuditEvent per token or text delta
- One long turn produces bounded telemetry events
- Final agent messages and all command/tool lifecycle events remain reconstructible
- Restart flushes aggregates without duplication or loss
- Audit chain remains RFC 8785-valid
- Full Symphony gate passes
